content.content:
1‚Üí# Kapow ‚Äî College Power Search: Product Specification
     2‚Üí
     3‚Üí## 1. Product Overview
     4‚Üí
     5‚Üí**Kapow (College Power Search)** is a web application that provides a comprehensive, faceted search engine for US colleges and universities. It aggregates data from 12 independent sources into a single searchable interface, enabling users to filter, sort, compare, and export institution data across 48 searchable dimensions.
     6‚Üí
     7‚Üí### Target Users
     8‚Üí
     9‚Üí- **Prospective students** researching colleges that fit their academic, financial, geographic, and lifestyle preferences
    10‚Üí- **Parents and families** comparing cost, outcomes, and value across institutions
    11‚Üí- **High school counselors** building filtered shortlists and exporting data for students
    12‚Üí- **AI assistants** querying college data programmatically via the MCP (Model Context Protocol) endpoint
    13‚Üí
    14‚Üí### Core Value Proposition
    15‚Üí
    16‚ÜíCollege Power Search consolidates data that is otherwise scattered across dozens of websites ‚Äî federal statistics, multiple ranking publications, athletic conferences, climate data, partisan lean scores, and application platform listings ‚Äî into a single interface with real-time faceted filtering. Users can combine any number of criteria simultaneously, view results in four different formats, create custom ranking blends from multiple sources, and export their findings as CSV or PDF.
    17‚Üí
    18‚Üí---
    19‚Üí
    20‚Üí## 2. Feature Inventory
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí### 2.1 Faceted Search Engine
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíThe search engine exposes 48 filterable fields organized into 11 active themes (with 7 additional theme slots reserved for future expansion). Every filter is applied server-side as a parameterized SQL WHERE clause against a denormalized materialized view (`dataset_merged`), ensuring fast queries across the full dataset.
    25‚Üí
    26‚Üí**Active themes and their fields:**
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üí| Theme | Fields | Description |
    29‚Üí|---|---|---|
    30‚Üí| Core | Institution Name (full-text), Unit ID | Name search across institution name, city, and state |
    31‚Üí| Location | City, State, Geographic Proximity, Locale, Region, Metro Area, Metro Distance | Where the school is and what kind of setting it occupies |
    32‚Üí| Academics | Institution Type, Highest Degree, Enrollment, Graduate Enrollment, Student-Faculty Ratio, Full-Time Faculty Rate, Gender (coed/single-sex), Online Only, Carnegie Classification | Academic profile and size |
    33‚Üí| Cost & Financial Aid | Avg Net Price, In-State Tuition, Out-of-State Tuition, Room & Board, Net Price (low-income), Pell Grant Rate, Federal Loan Rate, Endowment | What it costs and how it is funded |
    34‚Üí| Outcomes | Admission Rate, Graduation Rate, Retention Rate, Transfer Rate | Student success metrics |
    35‚Üí| Rankings | US News National, US News Liberal Arts, Forbes, Washington Monthly National, Washington Monthly Liberal Arts, Niche, Money Score | Rankings from 6 publications (7 lists) |
    36‚Üí| Admissions | Application Platforms (Common App, Coalition, Universal) | How to apply |
    37‚Üí| Athletics | NCAA Division, NCAA Conference | Athletic conference and competition level |
    38‚Üí| Fields of Study | CIP-coded program offerings | What majors and programs are available |
    39‚Üí| Cultural Environment | Partisan Direction, Partisan Score, Congressional District | Political leaning of the surrounding area |
    40‚Üí| Weather | Koppen Climate Group, Koppen Temperature Subtype, Koppen Precipitation Subtype | Climate classification of the campus location |
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí**Filter types supported:**
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí| Filter Type | Behavior | Example |
    45‚Üí|---|---|---|
    46‚Üí| Full-text search | PostgreSQL `tsvector` matching | "MIT" matches Massachusetts Institute of Technology |
    47‚Üí| Substring match | Case-insensitive partial match | "Arbor" matches Ann Arbor |
    48‚Üí| Single enum | Exact match from a fixed option list | Institution Type = "Public" |
    49‚Üí| Multi enum | Match any or all from a set | State in [CA, NY, MA] |
    50‚Üí| Numeric range | Min/max bounds on a numeric value | Tuition between $5,000 and $20,000 |
    51‚Üí| Exact string | Case-insensitive exact match | Metro Area = "Boston" |
    52‚Üí| Exact numeric | Exact integer match | Carnegie Classification = 15 |
    53‚Üí| Geographic proximity | PostGIS radius search | Within 50 miles of a given lat/lng |
    54‚Üí| Ranking | Numeric range on ranking position | US News rank 1-25 |
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí### 2.2 Four Result Views
    57‚Üí
    58‚ÜíUsers can switch between four ways to view search results:
    59‚Üí
    60‚Üí| View | Description |
    61‚Üí|---|---|
    62‚Üí| **Card** (default) | Responsive grid of summary cards showing key stats per institution |
    63‚Üí| **List** | Compact row-based list emphasizing quick scanning |
    64‚Üí| **Table** | Full data table with sortable columns, customizable column visibility, and drag-to-reorder |
    65‚Üí| **Map** | Leaflet-based map with institution markers; supports click-to-detail |
    66‚Üí
    67‚ÜíThe selected view mode persists in `localStorage` and can also be set via URL parameter (`?view=table`).
    68‚Üí
    69‚Üí### 2.3 Custom Ranking Blends
    70‚Üí
    71‚ÜíUsers can create weighted composite rankings by assigning weights to any combination of the 7 ranking fields. When a blend is active, results are sorted by the computed blend score rather than by a single column. Blend weights are serialized in the URL (`?blend=...`) so blended searches can be shared via link.
    72‚Üí
    73‚Üí### 2.4 Sorting and Pagination
    74‚Üí
    75‚Üí- Any field marked as `sortable` can be used as the sort column (ascending or descending)
    76‚Üí- When a user location is set, results can be sorted by distance
    77‚Üí- Pagination supports configurable page sizes (5, 10, 20, 50, 100) with a maximum of 100 results per search
    78‚Üí- All sort and pagination state is encoded in URL parameters for shareability
    79‚Üí
    80‚Üí### 2.5 Column Customization (Table View)
    81‚Üí
    82‚ÜíIn table view, users can:
    83‚Üí- Show or hide any output field using a column picker
    84‚Üí- Reorder columns via drag-and-drop or up/down controls
    85‚Üí- Column visibility and order persist in `localStorage`
    86‚Üí- Default-visible columns are determined by field metadata from the API
    87‚Üí
    88‚Üí### 2.6 Institution Detail
    89‚Üí
    90‚ÜíClicking any institution in any view opens a detail drawer/modal showing expanded information including:
    91‚Üí- Core identity (name, city, state, coordinates)
    92‚Üí- Institution type and enrollment
    93‚Üí- Admission and graduation rates
    94‚Üí- Tuition (in-state and out-of-state) and average net price
    95‚Üí- Retention rate and student-faculty ratio
    96‚Üí
    97‚Üí### 2.7 Similar Institutions
    98‚Üí
    99‚ÜíThe API provides a `similarInstitutions` query that, given a specific institution, returns other schools with similar profiles based on type, enrollment, admission rate, graduation rate, and tuition. Results include a similarity distance score.
   100‚Üí
   101‚Üí### 2.8 CSV and PDF Export
   102‚Üí
   103‚Üí- **CSV Export**: Server-side export via `/api/export/csv` that respects active filters, sort order, and user location. Downloads directly as a file. Includes all exportable fields.
   104‚Üí- **PDF Export**: Client-side PDF generation using the current result set, active output fields, and active filter labels. Includes data source attribution.
   105‚Üí
   106‚Üí### 2.9 Geographic Features
   107‚Üí
   108‚Üí- **Proximity search**: PostGIS-powered `ST_DWithin` queries with configurable radius (up to ~310 miles / 500 km)
   109‚Üí- **Distance calculation**: When a user location is set, each result includes its distance from that location
   110‚Üí- **My Price**: When a user's home state is known, the system returns in-state tuition for public schools in that state and out-of-state tuition otherwise
   111‚Üí- **Map view**: Interactive Leaflet map with markers for all results in the current page
   112‚Üí- **ZIP code resolution**: The `resolveZip` query resolves a US ZIP code to latitude/longitude and state, used by the `LocationInput` component for location-by-ZIP entry (queries `location.zip_coordinates`)
   113‚Üí
   114‚Üí### 2.10 Authentication and User Preferences
   115‚Üí
   116‚Üí- **Authentication**: Supabase-based authentication supporting Google, GitHub, and email sign-in via OAuth
   117‚Üí- **User profile**: The `me` query returns the authenticated user's ID, email, display name, avatar, and auth provider
   118‚Üí- **Preferences**: Authenticated users can save a home location (latitude, longitude, state) that automatically populates the user location for distance calculations and My Price computation
   119‚Üí
   120‚Üí### 2.11 Sample Searches
   121‚Üí
   122‚ÜíThe home page features a carousel of pre-built sample searches that demonstrate the system's filtering capabilities:
   123‚Üí
   124‚Üí| Sample | Filters Applied |
   125‚Üí|---|---|
   126‚Üí| Top 25 National | US News National rank <= 25 |
   127‚Üí| Affordable in California | State = CA, net price <= $20,000 |
   128‚Üí| D1 Small Schools | NCAA Division I, enrollment <= 5,000 |
   129‚Üí| Selective & Affordable | Admission rate <= 30%, net price <= $20,000 |
   130‚Üí| D3 New England | NCAA Division III, New England region |
   131‚Üí| Sunny & Affordable | Dry (Koppen B) climate, net price <= $15,000 |
   132‚Üí
   133‚ÜíEach sample search shows a live preview of matching institutions before the user navigates to the full search page.
   134‚Üí
   135‚Üí### 2.12 MCP (Model Context Protocol) Endpoint
   136‚Üí
   137‚ÜíThe application exposes a WebMCP interface that allows AI clients (such as Claude) to programmatically search the college database. Two tools are registered:
   138‚Üí
   139‚Üí| Tool | Description |
   140‚Üí|---|---|
   141‚Üí| `search_colleges` | Execute a filtered search with field/value pairs, limit, and offset |
   142‚Üí| `get_search_fields` | Retrieve the full field descriptor catalog including types and options |
   143‚Üí
   144‚Üí### 2.13 Data Source Attribution
   145‚Üí
   146‚ÜíEvery search field is linked to its originating data source. The UI can display source badges and tooltips, and the `dataSources` GraphQL query returns the full catalog with names, short names, URLs, and descriptions. This attribution carries through to PDF exports.
   147‚Üí
   148‚Üí---
   149‚Üí
   150‚Üí## 3. Pages
   151‚Üí
   152‚Üí| Route | Page | Description |
   153‚Üí|---|---|---|
   154‚Üí| `/` | Home | Landing page with hero search form (name, state, ranking, major), sample search carousel, and feature highlights |
   155‚Üí| `/search` | Search | Main search interface with collapsible filter sidebar (desktop: fixed 280px sidebar; mobile: toggle-to-show), results area with view mode toggle, export buttons, pagination, and institution detail drawer |
   156‚Üí| `/about` | About | Information page about the application |
   157‚Üí| `/auth/callback` | Auth Callback | OAuth redirect handler for Supabase authentication |
   158‚Üí
   159‚ÜíAll pages share a common `Header` (with navigation and auth controls) and `Footer`.
   160‚Üí
   161‚Üí---
   162‚Üí
   163‚Üí## 4. User Stories
   164‚Üí
   165‚Üí### Student
   166‚Üí
   167‚Üí- As a student, I want to search for colleges by name so I can quickly find a specific school.
   168‚Üí- As a student, I want to filter by state, cost, and admission rate simultaneously so I can find affordable schools I have a realistic chance of attending.
   169‚Üí- As a student, I want to filter by fields of study so I can find schools that offer my intended major.
   170‚Üí- As a student, I want to see how far each school is from my home so I can gauge travel time.
   171‚Üí- As a student, I want to view results on a map so I can see the geographic distribution of matching schools.
   172‚Üí- As a student, I want to create a custom ranking blend weighting cost and graduation rate so I can find the best value schools by my own criteria.
   173‚Üí
   174‚Üí### Parent
   175‚Üí
   176‚Üí- As a parent, I want to compare in-state and out-of-state tuition so I can understand the true cost.
   177‚Üí- As a parent, I want to see my personalized net price (My Price) so I know what public schools in my state would actually cost.
   178‚Üí- As a parent, I want to sort by graduation rate so I can prioritize schools where students succeed.
   179‚Üí- As a parent, I want to export the shortlist as a PDF so I can share it with my student.
   180‚Üí
   181‚Üí### Counselor
   182‚Üí
   183‚Üí- As a counselor, I want to export a filtered list as CSV so I can analyze it in a spreadsheet.
   184‚Üí- As a counselor, I want to use the table view with custom columns so I can compare specific metrics across many schools.
   185‚Üí- As a counselor, I want to share a search via URL so I can send a pre-filtered view to a student or family.
   186‚Üí- As a counselor, I want to use sample searches as starting points for common student profiles.
   187‚Üí
   188‚Üí### Authenticated User
   189‚Üí
   190‚Üí- As a returning user, I want to save my home location so distance and My Price are calculated automatically on every visit.
   191‚Üí- As a user, I want my column layout and view mode preferences to persist between sessions.
   192‚Üí
   193‚Üí### AI Client
   194‚Üí
   195‚Üí- As an AI assistant, I want to query available search fields so I can understand what filters exist.
   196‚Üí- As an AI assistant, I want to search colleges with structured filters so I can answer user questions about college options.
   197‚Üí
   198‚Üí---
   199‚Üí
   200‚Üí## 5. Data Sources
   201‚Üí
   202‚Üí| # | Source | Short Name | Data Provided | URL |
   203‚Üí|---|---|---|---|---|
   204‚Üí| 1 | National Center for Education Statistics (IPEDS) | NCES | Academics, cost, outcomes, enrollment, demographics, retention, graduation rates, faculty ratios, endowments | nces.ed.gov/ipeds |
   205‚Üí| 2 | U.S. News & World Report ‚Äî National Universities | US News | National university rankings | usnews.com |
   206‚Üí| 3 | U.S. News & World Report ‚Äî Liberal Arts Colleges | US News | Liberal arts college rankings | usnews.com |
   207‚Üí| 4 | Forbes America's Top Colleges | Forbes | Overall college rankings | forbes.com |
   208‚Üí| 5 | Washington Monthly College Rankings | Wash Monthly | Rankings emphasizing social mobility, research, and public service (national + liberal arts) | washingtonmonthly.com |
   209‚Üí| 6 | Niche Best Colleges | Niche | Rankings based on academics, value, and student reviews | niche.com |
   210‚Üí| 7 | Money Magazine Best Colleges | Money | College value scores balancing quality, affordability, and outcomes | money.com |
   211‚Üí| 8 | National Collegiate Athletic Association | NCAA | Division and conference data for intercollegiate athletics | ncaa.org |
   212‚Üí| 9 | FiveThirtyEight Partisan Lean | 538 | Congressional-district-level partisan lean direction and scores | fivethirtyeight.com |
   213‚Üí| 10 | Koppen Climate Classification | Koppen | Climate group, temperature subtype, and precipitation subtype | wikipedia.org (Koppen) |
   214‚Üí| 11 | College Application Platforms | Apps | Which platforms each school accepts (Common App, Coalition, Universal) | commonapp.org |
   215‚Üí| 12 | U.S. Census Bureau | Census | Metropolitan area names, distances, and IPEDS locale classifications | census.gov |
   216‚Üí
   217‚Üí---
   218‚Üí
   219‚Üí## 6. Architecture Overview
   220‚Üí
   221‚ÜíKapow is a monorepo with a Go API backend, a React frontend, and a PostgreSQL
   222‚Üídatabase. For detailed architecture documentation, see:
   223‚Üí
   224‚Üí- **[Frontend Architecture](frontend-architecture.md)** ‚Äî tech stack, components, state management, GraphQL integration
   225‚Üí- **[GraphQL API](graphql-api.md)** ‚Äî queries, mutations, types, request lifecycle
   226‚Üí- **[Search Engine](search-engine.md)** ‚Äî field types, SQL generation, themes, data sources
   227‚Üí- **[Data Model](data-model.md)** ‚Äî schemas, tables, relationships, migrations
   228‚Üí- **[Authentication](authentication.md)** ‚Äî JWT flow, middleware, Supabase integration
   229‚Üí- **[Deployment](deployment.md)** ‚Äî Docker images, Compose, env vars, startup sequence
   230‚Üí

1‚Üí# GraphQL API Specification
     2‚Üí
     3‚ÜíKapow exposes a single GraphQL endpoint at `/graphql` (POST and GET).
     4‚ÜíThis document describes the complete API contract that clients need to
     5‚Üíconsume the service. For search field details, see
     6‚Üí[search-engine.md](search-engine.md). For authentication flow, see
     7‚Üí[authentication.md](authentication.md).
     8‚Üí
     9‚Üí---
    10‚Üí
    11‚Üí## Endpoint
    12‚Üí
    13‚Üí```
    14‚ÜíPOST /graphql
    15‚ÜíGET  /graphql   (query via query-string ‚Äî primarily for GraphiQL)
    16‚Üí```
    17‚Üí
    18‚Üí**Content-Type**: `application/json`
    19‚Üí
    20‚Üí**Authentication**: OptionalAuth. Anonymous requests are accepted and can
    21‚Üíexecute public queries. If a valid `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header is
    22‚Üípresent, the user is provisioned (team + membership created) and claims are
    23‚Üíavailable to resolvers. Invalid or malformed tokens are silently ignored;
    24‚Üíthe request proceeds as unauthenticated.
    25‚Üí
    26‚Üí---
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üí## Request Lifecycle
    29‚Üí
    30‚ÜíA GraphQL request to `/graphql` traverses the following layers:
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üí1. **Global middleware** (applied to all routes, in order):
    33‚Üí   - `OTel` (conditional) ‚Äî when `OTEL_ENABLED=true`, `otelhttp` middleware records
    34‚Üí     HTTP metrics (duration, count by method/status) and creates a trace span per request
    35‚Üí   - `RequestID` ‚Äî generates or propagates `X-Request-ID`
    36‚Üí   - `Logging` ‚Äî structured slog request/response logging
    37‚Üí   - `Recovery` ‚Äî panic recovery ‚Üí 500 response
    38‚Üí   - `HTTPDetail` ‚Äî optional HTTP debug header/body logging
    39‚Üí   - `RealIP` (chi) ‚Äî resolves client IP from proxy headers
    40‚Üí   - `StripSlashes` (chi) ‚Äî normalizes trailing slashes
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí2. **Route middleware** (applied to `/graphql` group):
    43‚Üí   - `OptionalAuth` ‚Äî if `Authorization: Bearer <token>` is present and valid,
    44‚Üí     stores `*auth.Claims` in context; if absent, passes through anonymously;
    45‚Üí     if present and invalid, returns 401
    46‚Üí   - `EnsureUser` ‚Äî if claims exist, idempotently creates a personal team +
    47‚Üí     team membership row via `user.Service.EnsureUser`
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí3. **gqlgen handler** ‚Äî parses the GraphQL operation, validates it against the
    50‚Üí   schema, and dispatches to the appropriate resolver method on the `Resolver`
    51‚Üí   struct (which holds `DB`, `Search` registry, and `Preference` service)
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üí4. **Resolver** ‚Äî executes business logic:
    54‚Üí   - Search queries call `ValidateRequest()` ‚Üí `BuildQuery()` / `BuildCountQuery()`
    55‚Üí   - SQL is executed via `pgxpool.Pool.Query()` with parameterized placeholders
    56‚Üí   - Results are serialized into the GraphQL response types
    57‚Üí
    58‚Üí5. **Response** ‚Äî gqlgen serializes the response as JSON and returns it with
    59‚Üí   HTTP 200 (even for GraphQL-level errors; see [Error Handling](#error-handling))
    60‚Üí
    61‚ÜíFor `/mcp/*` routes, the middleware stack replaces `OptionalAuth` with
    62‚Üímandatory `Auth` ‚Äî unauthenticated requests receive a 401 with a
    63‚Üí`WWW-Authenticate` header. See [authentication.md](authentication.md) for the
    64‚Üícomplete route-level auth table.
    65‚Üí
    66‚Üí---
    67‚Üí
    68‚Üí## Queries
    69‚Üí
    70‚Üí### echo
    71‚Üí
    72‚ÜíHealth-check / connectivity test.
    73‚Üí
    74‚Üí```graphql
    75‚Üíquery {
    76‚Üí  echo(message: "hello") {
    77‚Üí    message
    78‚Üí    receivedAt
    79‚Üí  }
    80‚Üí}
    81‚Üí```
    82‚Üí
    83‚Üí| Argument | Type | Required | Description |
    84‚Üí|----------|------|----------|-------------|
    85‚Üí| `message` | `String!` | yes | Arbitrary string echoed back |
    86‚Üí
    87‚Üí**Returns**: `EchoResponse!`
    88‚Üí
    89‚Üí| Field | Type | Description |
    90‚Üí|-------|------|-------------|
    91‚Üí| `message` | `String!` | The input message, echoed verbatim |
    92‚Üí| `receivedAt` | `String!` | Server timestamp (ISO 8601) |
    93‚Üí
    94‚Üí**Auth**: not required.
    95‚Üí
    96‚Üí---
    97‚Üí
    98‚Üí### me
    99‚Üí
   100‚ÜíReturns the authenticated user's profile, derived from JWT claims.
   101‚Üí
   102‚Üí```graphql
   103‚Üíquery {
   104‚Üí  me {
   105‚Üí    id
   106‚Üí    email
   107‚Üí    fullName
   108‚Üí    avatarUrl
   109‚Üí    provider
   110‚Üí  }
   111‚Üí}
   112‚Üí```
   113‚Üí
   114‚Üí**Returns**: `User` (nullable). Returns `null` when no valid token is present.
   115‚Üí
   116‚Üí| Field | Type | Description |
   117‚Üí|-------|------|-------------|
   118‚Üí| `id` | `ID!` | Supabase user UUID (`sub` claim) |
   119‚Üí| `email` | `String!` | User email |
   120‚Üí| `fullName` | `String` | Display name from identity provider |
   121‚Üí| `avatarUrl` | `String` | Profile image URL |
   122‚Üí| `provider` | `String` | Auth provider (e.g. `google`, `github`) |
   123‚Üí
   124‚Üí**Auth**: not required, but returns `null` without a valid token.
   125‚Üí
   126‚Üí---
   127‚Üí
   128‚Üí### search
   129‚Üí
   130‚ÜíPrimary search query. Accepts filters, pagination, sorting, user location
   131‚Üí(for distance calculations), and optional ranking blend weights.
   132‚Üí
   133‚Üí```graphql
   134‚Üíquery Search($filters: [FilterInput!], $limit: Int, $offset: Int) {
   135‚Üí  search(filters: $filters, limit: $limit, offset: $offset) {
   136‚Üí    total
   137‚Üí    limit
   138‚Üí    offset
   139‚Üí    results {
   140‚Üí      unitID
   141‚Üí      name
   142‚Üí      city
   143‚Üí      state
   144‚Üí      distance
   145‚Üí      myPrice
   146‚Üí      blendScore
   147‚Üí      admissions { applicationPlatforms }
   148‚Üí      rankings { usNewsNational forbes }
   149‚Üí    }
   150‚Üí  }
   151‚Üí}
   152‚Üí```
   153‚Üí
   154‚Üí| Argument | Type | Required | Description |
   155‚Üí|----------|------|----------|-------------|

   298‚Üí### searchFields
   299‚Üí
   300‚ÜíReturns all available search/filter fields with metadata describing their type,
   301‚Üívalidation constraints, and UI grouping. Clients use this to dynamically render
   302‚Üífilter controls. See [search-engine.md](search-engine.md) for field details.
   303‚Üí
   304‚Üí```graphql
   305‚Üíquery {
   306‚Üí  searchFields {
   307‚Üí    name
   308‚Üí    label
   309‚Üí    description
   310‚Üí    theme
   311‚Üí    visibility
   312‚Üí    searchability
   313‚Üí    ... on ChoiceSearchField { options { value displayName } }
   314‚Üí    ... on NumericalRangeSearchField { min max step unit }
   315‚Üí    ... on GeoSearchField { defaultLatitude defaultLongitude maxRadiusMiles }
   316‚Üí  }
   317‚Üí}
   318‚Üí```
   319‚Üí
   320‚Üí**Returns**: `[SearchField!]!`
   321‚Üí
   322‚Üí`SearchField` is an interface implemented by six concrete types. All share
   323‚Üíthese base fields:
   324‚Üí
   325‚Üí| Field | Type | Description |
   326‚Üí|-------|------|-------------|
   327‚Üí| `name` | `String!` | Filter key used in `FilterInput.field` |
   328‚Üí| `label` | `String!` | Human-readable display label |
   329‚Üí| `description` | `String!` | Help text |
   330‚Üí| `theme` | `Theme!` | UI grouping category |
   331‚Üí| `visibility` | `FieldVisibility!` | Display visibility level |
   332‚Üí| `searchability` | `FieldSearchability!` | Whether the field is filterable |
   333‚Üí| `exportable` | `Boolean!` | Whether the field can be exported |
   334‚Üí| `dataSourceID` | `String` | ID of the originating data source |
   335‚Üí
   336‚Üí#### SearchField implementations
   337‚Üí
   338‚Üí| Type | Extra fields | Filter value format |
   339‚Üí|------|-------------|---------------------|
   340‚Üí| `TextSearchField` | `minLength`, `maxLength`, `pattern` | `"search term"` (string) |
   341‚Üí| `BinarySearchField` | (none) | `"1"` (string) |
   342‚Üí| `ChoiceSearchField` | `options`, `minSelections`, `maxSelections` | `["val1", "val2"]` (string array) |
   343‚Üí| `NumericalSearchField` | `min`, `max`, `step`, `unit`, `defaultValue` | `42` (number) |
   344‚Üí| `NumericalRangeSearchField` | `min`, `max`, `step`, `unit`, `defaultLower`, `defaultUpper`, `minSpan`, `maxSpan` | `[10, 90]` (two-element number array) |
   345‚Üí| `GeoSearchField` | `defaultLatitude`, `defaultLongitude`, `maxRadiusMiles` | `{"latitude": 40.7, "longitude": -74.0, "radiusMiles": 50}` |
   346‚Üí
   347‚Üí**Auth**: not required.
   348‚Üí

   523‚Üí## Input Types
   524‚Üí
   525‚Üí### FilterInput
   526‚Üí
   527‚ÜíUsed in the `search` query to apply filters.
   528‚Üí
   529‚Üí```json
   530‚Üí{ "field": "state", "value": ["CA", "NY"] }
   531‚Üí```
   532‚Üí
   533‚Üí| Field | Type | Description |
   534‚Üí|-------|------|-------------|
   535‚Üí| `field` | `SearchFieldName!` | Field identifier (enum) |
   536‚Üí| `value` | `FilterValue!` | Filter value (type depends on field) |
   537‚Üí
   538‚Üí`FilterValue` is a custom scalar backed by arbitrary JSON. The expected shape
   539‚Üídepends on the field type -- see the "Filter value format" column in the
   540‚Üí[SearchField implementations](#searchfield-implementations) table above.
   541‚Üí
   542‚Üí### SortInput
   543‚Üí
   544‚Üí```json
   545‚Üí{ "field": "name", "direction": "ASC" }
   546‚Üí```
   547‚Üí
   548‚Üí| Field | Type | Description |
   549‚Üí|-------|------|-------------|
   550‚Üí| `field` | `SearchFieldName!` | Field to sort by |
   551‚Üí| `direction` | `SortDirection` | `ASC` (default) or `DESC` |
   552‚Üí
   553‚Üí### UserLocation
   554‚Üí
   555‚ÜíProvides the user's coordinates for distance calculation and in-state

   558‚Üí```json
   559‚Üí{ "latitude": 40.7128, "longitude": -74.006, "state": "NY" }
   560‚Üí```

   562‚Üí| Field | Type | Description |
   563‚Üí|-------|------|-------------|
   564‚Üí| `latitude` | `Float!` | User latitude |
   565‚Üí| `longitude` | `Float!` | User longitude |
   566‚Üí| `state` | `String` | User's home state (for `myPrice` calculation) |
   567‚Üí
   568‚Üí### RankingBlendWeight
   569‚Üí
   570‚ÜíDefines a weight for a ranking field in a custom composite score.
   571‚Üí
   572‚Üí```json
   573‚Üí{ "field": "rank_us_news_national", "weight": 0.5 }

   582‚Üí
   583‚Üí## Enums
   584‚Üí
   585‚Üí### Theme
   586‚Üí
   587‚ÜíGroups search fields by category for UI organization.
   588‚Üí
   589‚Üí```
   590‚ÜíCORE, LOCATION, ACADEMICS, COST, OUTCOMES, RANKING, ADMISSIONS,
   591‚ÜíATHLETICS, FIELDS_OF_STUDY, CULTURAL_ENVIRONMENT, WEATHER,
   592‚ÜíPHYSICAL_ENVIRONMENT, DEMOGRAPHICS, REPUTATION, SAFETY,
   593‚ÜíSTUDENT_LIFE, METADATA, OTHER
   594‚Üí```
   595‚Üí
   596‚Üí### SearchFieldName
   597‚Üí
   598‚ÜíAll 48 registered search field identifiers. Auto-generated from the field
   599‚Üíregistry -- see `api/graph/schema/field_names_gen.graphql` for the full list.
   600‚ÜíNotable fields: `name`, `state`, `location` (geo), `enrollment`,
   601‚Üí`admission_rate`, `graduation_rate`, `tuition_in_state`, `distance`,
   602‚Üí`rank_us_news_national`, `fields_of_study`.
   603‚Üí
   604‚Üí### FieldVisibility
   605‚Üí
   606‚ÜíControls whether a field is shown in the UI.
   607‚Üí
   608‚Üí| Value | Description |
   609‚Üí|-------|-------------|
   610‚Üí| `VISIBLE` | Shown to all users |
   611‚Üí| `RESTRICTED` | Shown only to authorized users |
   612‚Üí| `HIDDEN` | Not shown in the UI |
   613‚Üí
   614‚Üí> **Note**: `RESTRICTED` is defined in the enum but **not currently enforced**.
   615‚Üí> No fields use it, and no auth checks exist in resolvers or validation to
   616‚Üí> filter fields by user role. It is a placeholder for future role-based access.
   617‚Üí
   618‚Üí### FieldSearchability
   619‚Üí
   620‚ÜíControls whether a field can be used as a filter.
   621‚Üí
   622‚Üí| Value | Description |
   623‚Üí|-------|-------------|
   624‚Üí| `SEARCHABLE` | Available as a filter |
   625‚Üí| `RESTRICTED` | Filter restricted to authorized users |
   626‚Üí| `NOT_SEARCHABLE` | Output-only; cannot be used as a filter |
   627‚Üí
   628‚Üí> **Note**: `RESTRICTED` is defined but not enforced ‚Äî same caveat as
   629‚Üí> `FieldVisibility.RESTRICTED` above.
   630‚Üí
   631‚Üí### SortDirection
   632‚Üí
   633‚Üí| Value | Description |
   634‚Üí|-------|-------------|
   635‚Üí| `ASC` | Ascending |
   636‚Üí| `DESC` | Descending |
   637‚Üí
   638‚Üí### SearchFieldUnit
   639‚Üí
   640‚ÜíDisplay format hint for numerical fields.
   641‚Üí
   642‚Üí```
   643‚ÜíDOLLARS, PERCENTAGE, MILES, HOURS, MINUTES
   644‚Üí```
   645‚Üí
   646‚Üí---
   647‚Üí
   648‚Üí## Error Handling
   649‚Üí
   650‚ÜíAll resolver errors are returned as plain strings in `errors[].message`. The
   651‚ÜíAPI does **not** use `extensions.code` ‚Äî there are no structured error codes.
   652‚ÜíThe HTTP status is always **200** for GraphQL-level errors; **401** is only
   653‚Üíreturned by middleware (e.g., invalid token on `/mcp/*`).
   654‚Üí
   655‚Üí### Error response format
   656‚Üí
   657‚Üí```json
   658‚Üí{
   659‚Üí  "data": null,
   660‚Üí  "errors": [
   661‚Üí    {
   662‚Üí      "message": "preferences: authentication required",
   663‚Üí      "path": ["preferences"]
   664‚Üí    }
   665‚Üí  ]
   666‚Üí}
   667‚Üí```
   668‚Üí
   669‚Üí### Error categories
   670‚Üí
   671‚Üí| Category | Message pattern | Example |
   672‚Üí|----------|----------------|---------|
   673‚Üí| Auth required | `"<resolver>: authentication required"` | `"preferences: authentication required"`, `"updatePreferences: authentication required"` |
   674‚Üí| Search validation | `"search: validation: field \"<name>\": <detail>"` | `"search: validation: field \"enrollment\": min must be <= max"` |
   675‚Üí| Search validation (global) | `"search: validation: <detail>"` | `"search: validation: limit must not exceed 100"` |
   676‚Üí| Database / internal | `"search: count query: <detail>"`, `"search: query: <detail>"` | `"search: query: ERROR: ..."` |
   677‚Üí| Not found | `null` data field | `institution(unitID: "invalid")` returns `{"data": {"institution": null}}` |
   678‚Üí
   679‚Üí### Validation errors
   680‚Üí
   681‚Üí`ValidateRequest()` in the search engine collects all validation errors without
   682‚Üíshort-circuiting. Each error has a `Field` (the filter key or a sentinel like
   683‚Üí`_limit`, `_offset`) and a `Message`. The resolver surfaces only the **first**
   684‚Üívalidation error as a GraphQL error string in the format shown above.
   685‚Üí
   686‚Üí---
   687‚Üí
   688‚Üí## Usage Examples
   689‚Üí
   690‚Üí### Filtered search with pagination
   691‚Üí
   692‚Üí```graphql
   693‚Üíquery FilteredSearch {
   694‚Üí  search(
   695‚Üí    filters: [
   696‚Üí      { field: state, value: ["CA", "NY", "MA"] }
   697‚Üí      { field: enrollment, value: [1000, 10000] }
   698‚Üí      { field: admission_rate, value: [0, 0.3] }
   699‚Üí    ]
   700‚Üí    limit: 25
   701‚Üí    offset: 0
   702‚Üí    sort: { field: name, direction: ASC }
   703‚Üí    userLocation: { latitude: 40.7128, longitude: -74.006, state: "NY" }
   704‚Üí  ) {
   705‚Üí    total
   706‚Üí    results {
   707‚Üí      unitID
   708‚Üí      name
   709‚Üí      state
   710‚Üí      distance
   711‚Üí      myPrice
   712‚Üí      outcomes { admissionRate graduationRate }
   713‚Üí      cost { avgNetPrice tuitionInState tuitionOutOfState }
   714‚Üí      rankings { usNewsNational forbes }
   715‚Üí    }
   716‚Üí  }
   717‚Üí}
   718‚Üí```
   719‚Üí
   720‚Üí### Custom ranking blend
   721‚Üí
   722‚Üí```graphql
   723‚Üíquery BlendedSearch {
   724‚Üí  search(
   725‚Üí    rankingBlend: [
   726‚Üí      { field: rank_us_news_national, weight: 0.4 }
   727‚Üí      { field: rank_forbes, weight: 0.3 }
   728‚Üí      { field: score_money, weight: 0.3 }
   729‚Üí    ]
   730‚Üí    limit: 20
   731‚Üí  ) {
   732‚Üí    results {
   733‚Üí      name
   734‚Üí      blendScore
   735‚Üí      rankings { usNewsNational forbes moneyScore }
   736‚Üí    }
   737‚Üí  }
   738‚Üí}

   740‚Üí
   741‚Üí### Dynamic filter UI bootstrap
   742‚Üí
   743‚Üí```graphql
   744‚Üíquery BootstrapFilters {
   745‚Üí  searchFields {
   746‚Üí    name
   747‚Üí    label
   748‚Üí    description
   749‚Üí    theme
   750‚Üí    visibility
   751‚Üí    searchability
   752‚Üí    ... on TextSearchField { minLength maxLength }
   753‚Üí    ... on ChoiceSearchField {
   754‚Üí      options { value displayName }
   755‚Üí      minSelections
   756‚Üí      maxSelections
   757‚Üí    }
   758‚Üí    ... on NumericalRangeSearchField {
   759‚Üí      min max step unit defaultLower defaultUpper
   760‚Üí    }
   761‚Üí    ... on GeoSearchField {
   762‚Üí      defaultLatitude defaultLongitude maxRadiusMiles
   763‚Üí    }
   764‚Üí  }
   765‚Üí  searchOutputFields {
   766‚Üí    name label defaultVisible sortable unit outputKey
   767‚Üí  }
   768‚Üí  dataSources {
   769‚Üí    id name url fields
   770‚Üí  }
   771‚Üí}
   772‚Üí```

1‚Üí# Search Engine Specification
     2‚Üí
     3‚ÜíThe Kapow search engine lives in `api/internal/search/`. It translates typed
     4‚ÜíJSON filter payloads into parameterized SQL WHERE clauses executed against the
     5‚Üí`search_base.dataset_merged` materialized view. The engine is entirely
     6‚Üístateless: no in-process caches, no background workers. Every request produces
     7‚Üía fresh SQL query, and PostgreSQL indexes do the heavy lifting.
     8‚Üí
     9‚Üí---
    10‚Üí
    11‚Üí## Package Layout
    12‚Üí
    13‚Üí| File | Responsibility |
    14‚Üí|------|---------------|
    15‚Üí| `registry.go` | Field registry: `NewRegistry()`, `allFields()`, `Lookup()`, `Descriptors()`, `OutputDescriptors()`, `ExportableDescriptors()` |
    16‚Üí| `field.go` | `Field` interface, `baseField` embedding, concrete field type structs, `FieldDescriptor` metadata, `Theme` enum, `FieldVisibility`, `FieldSearchability` |
    17‚Üí| `kind.go` | `FacetKind` constants (`full_text`, `numeric_range`, `geo`, etc.) |
    18‚Üí| `source.go` | `FieldSource` struct, `SourceKind` constants, `SQLRef()` / `SQLRefAs()` helpers, JSONB value type mapping |
    19‚Üí| `filter.go` | Filter input structs deserialized from JSON (`FullTextFilter`, `NumericRangeFilter`, `GeoFilterInput`, etc.) |
    20‚Üí| `query.go` | `FilterInput`, `SearchRequest`, `SearchResult`, `SearchResponse` types; pagination constants |
    21‚Üí| `sql.go` | `BuildQuery()`, `BuildCountQuery()`, `BuildExportQuery()`, `BuildInstitutionQuery()`, `buildWhere()`, `buildOrderBy()` |
    22‚Üí| `validate.go` | `ValidateRequest()` -- collects all validation errors without short-circuiting |
    23‚Üí| `blend.go` | `BlendInput`, `BlendWeight`, `RankingProvider`, `buildBlendExpression()` |
    24‚Üí| `similarity.go` | `BuildSimilarInstitutionsQuery()` -- Euclidean distance similarity search |
    25‚Üí| `datasource.go` | `DataSource` struct and the catalog of 12 data sources |
    26‚Üí| `field_name_gen.go` | Generated field name constants |
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üí---
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üí## Core Abstractions
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üí### Field Interface
    33‚Üí
    34‚ÜíEvery search field implements the `Field` interface:
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üí```go
    37‚Üítype Field interface {
    38‚Üí    Name() string
    39‚Üí    Label() string
    40‚Üí    Description() string
    41‚Üí    Kind() FacetKind
    42‚Üí    Theme() Theme
    43‚Üí    Source() FieldSource
    44‚Üí    Descriptor() FieldDescriptor
    45‚Üí    BuildSQL(rawFilter json.RawMessage, argStart int) (expr string, args []any, err error)
    46‚Üí}
    47‚Üí```
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí`BuildSQL` is the central method. It receives the raw JSON filter payload and
    50‚Üíthe 1-based placeholder index, and returns a boolean SQL expression with
    51‚Üícorresponding bind arguments. The caller chains these expressions with AND.
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üí### FieldDescriptor
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí`FieldDescriptor` is the JSON-serializable metadata contract consumed by both
    56‚Üíthe GraphQL `searchFields` query and the REST metadata endpoint:
    57‚Üí
    58‚Üí| Property | Type | Purpose |
    59‚Üí|----------|------|---------|
    60‚Üí| `Name` | string | Filter key in API requests |
    61‚Üí| `Label` | string | Human-readable display name |
    62‚Üí| `Description` | string | Tooltip / help text |
    63‚Üí| `Kind` | FacetKind | Determines which filter UI to render |
    64‚Üí| `Theme` | Theme | UI grouping category |
    65‚Üí| `Options` | []EnumOption | Allowed values for enum fields |
    66‚Üí| `Bounds` | *NumericBounds | Hard min/max for numeric fields; max radius for geo fields |
    67‚Üí| `Units` | string | Display unit (USD, students, miles, rank) |
    68‚Üí| `SelectionMode` | SelectionMode | `any` or `all` for multi-enum |
    69‚Üí| `Visibility` | FieldVisibility | `VISIBLE`, `RESTRICTED`, or `HIDDEN` |
    70‚Üí| `Searchability` | FieldSearchability | `SEARCHABLE`, `RESTRICTED`, or `NOT_SEARCHABLE` |
    71‚Üí
    72‚Üí> **Note on RESTRICTED**: The `RESTRICTED` value is defined in both
    73‚Üí> `FieldVisibility` and `FieldSearchability` enums but is **not currently
    74‚Üí> enforced**. No fields use `RESTRICTED`, and no auth checks exist in
    75‚Üí> `ValidateRequest`, `BuildQuery`, or the field metadata resolvers to filter
    76‚Üí> fields by user role. It is a placeholder for future role-based field access.
    77‚Üí| `Exportable` | *bool | Whether the field can appear in CSV exports |
    78‚Üí| `OutputDefault` | *bool | `nil` = not an output field; `true` = shown by default; `false` = available in column picker |
    79‚Üí| `Sortable` | bool | Whether results can be sorted by this field |
    80‚Üí| `OutputKey` | string | Dot-path into the data JSONB blob for value extraction |
    81‚Üí| `DataSourceID` | string | References the originating data source |
    82‚Üí
    83‚Üí### baseField Embedding
    84‚Üí
    85‚ÜíAll concrete field types embed `baseField`, which provides default
    86‚Üíimplementations of `Name()`, `Label()`, `Description()`, `Kind()`, `Theme()`,
    87‚Üí`Source()`, and `Descriptor()`. Fields that need extra descriptor properties
    88‚Üí(Options, Bounds, Units) override `Descriptor()` and extend the base result.
    89‚Üí
    90‚Üí---
    91‚Üí
    92‚Üí## Field Types
    93‚Üí
    94‚Üí### FullTextSearchField
    95‚Üí
    96‚Üí| Property | Value |
    97‚Üí|----------|-------|
    98‚Üí| FacetKind | `full_text` |
    99‚Üí| Filter struct | `FullTextFilter { Query string }` |
   100‚Üí| SQL pattern | `tsv_col @@ plainto_tsquery('english', $N)` |
   101‚Üí| Use case | Institution name search across name, city, and state |
   102‚Üí
   103‚ÜíUses PostgreSQL `plainto_tsquery` for natural-language tokenization. The source
   104‚Üíis a TSVector column (`name_location_search_vector`) maintained by the
   105‚Üímaterialized view.
   106‚Üí
   107‚Üí### SubstringField
   108‚Üí
   109‚Üí| Property | Value |
   110‚Üí|----------|-------|
   111‚Üí| FacetKind | `substring` |
   112‚Üí| Filter struct | `SubstringFilter { Value string }` |
   113‚Üí| SQL pattern | `ref ILIKE '%' \|\| $N \|\| '%'` |
   114‚Üí| Use case | Partial text matching on city, conference name |
   115‚Üí
   116‚ÜíFor JSONB sources, a NULL guard is prepended: `ref IS NOT NULL AND ...`.
   117‚Üí
   118‚Üí### ExactStringField
   119‚Üí
   120‚Üí| Property | Value |
   121‚Üí|----------|-------|
   122‚Üí| FacetKind | `exact_string` |
   123‚Üí| Filter struct | `ExactStringFilter { Value string }` |
   124‚Üí| SQL pattern | `ref = $N` (case-sensitive) or `ref ILIKE $N` (case-insensitive) |
   125‚Üí| Use case | Unit ID lookup, metro area name match |
   126‚Üí
   127‚ÜíThe `CaseSensitive` flag on the struct controls the operator choice.
   128‚Üí
   129‚Üí### ExactNumericField
   130‚Üí
   131‚Üí| Property | Value |
   132‚Üí|----------|-------|
   133‚Üí| FacetKind | `exact_numeric` |
   134‚Üí| Filter struct | `ExactNumericFilter { Value float64 }` |
   135‚Üí| SQL pattern | `(ref)::integer = $N` (JSONB) or `col = $N` (column) |
   136‚Üí| Use case | Carnegie classification code, congressional district |
   137‚Üí
   138‚ÜíFor JSONB sources the extracted text is cast to the field's declared value type.
   139‚Üí
   140‚Üí### NumericRangeField
   141‚Üí
   142‚Üí| Property | Value |
   143‚Üí|----------|-------|
   144‚Üí| FacetKind | `numeric_range` |
   145‚Üí| Filter struct | `NumericRangeFilter { Min *float64, Max *float64 }` |
   146‚Üí| SQL pattern | `ref BETWEEN $N AND $M`, `ref >= $N`, or `ref <= $N` |
   147‚Üí| Use case | Tuition, enrollment, acceptance rate, scores |
   148‚Üí
   149‚ÜíAt least one of Min or Max must be provided. Optional `HardMin` / `HardMax`
   150‚Üíbounds are enforced during validation. The field carries `Units` (USD,
   151‚Üístudents, etc.) for display.
   152‚Üí
   153‚Üí### RankingField
   154‚Üí
   155‚Üí| Property | Value |
   156‚Üí|----------|-------|
   157‚Üí| FacetKind | `numeric_range` |
   158‚Üí| Filter struct | `NumericRangeFilter` (same as NumericRangeField) |
   159‚Üí| SQL pattern | `(data #>> '{key,method}') = 'rank' AND (ref) IS NOT NULL AND (ref)::integer BETWEEN $N AND $M` |
   160‚Üí| Use case | US News, Forbes, Washington Monthly, Niche ranking ranges |
   161‚Üí
   162‚ÜíEmbeds `NumericRangeField` but overrides `BuildSQL` to add a method guard.
   163‚ÜíOnly rows where the ranking dataset's `method` key equals `'rank'` are
   164‚Üíincluded, preventing score-based entries from matching rank-based filters.
   165‚Üí
   166‚Üí### GeoField
   167‚Üí
   168‚Üí| Property | Value |
   169‚Üí|----------|-------|
   170‚Üí| FacetKind | `geo` |
   171‚Üí| Filter struct | `GeoFilterInput { Kind, Radius?, BBox? }` |
   172‚Üí| SQL patterns | Radius: `ST_DWithin(col, ST_SetSRID(ST_MakePoint($lng, $lat), 4326), $km * 1000)` |
   173‚Üí| | BBox: `ST_Intersects(col, ST_MakeEnvelope($minLng, $minLat, $maxLng, $maxLat, 4326))` |
   174‚Üí| Use case | "Colleges within 50 km of Boston" |
   175‚Üí
   176‚ÜíSupports two sub-kinds via a discriminated union. The `MaxRadiusKm` field
   177‚Üí(currently 500 km) is enforced during validation. Coordinates use SRID 4326.
   178‚ÜíRadius distances are in metres internally (km * 1000).
   179‚Üí
   180‚Üí### SingleEnumField
   181‚Üí
   182‚Üí| Property | Value |
   183‚Üí|----------|-------|
   184‚Üí| FacetKind | `single_enum` |
   185‚Üí| Filter struct | `SingleEnumFilter { Value string }` |
   186‚Üí| SQL pattern | `ref = $N` |
   187‚Üí| Use case | State, region, institution type, NCAA division, climate |
   188‚Üí
   189‚ÜíCarries a fixed `Options` slice for validation and UI rendering. Validation
   190‚Üírejects values not in the options list.
   191‚Üí
   192‚Üí### GenderField
   193‚Üí
   194‚Üí| Property | Value |
   195‚Üí|----------|-------|
   196‚Üí| FacetKind | `single_enum` (virtual) |
   197‚Üí| Filter struct | `SingleEnumFilter { Value string }` |
   198‚Üí| SQL pattern | Varies by value: `men-only`, `women-only`, or `coed` |
   199‚Üí| Use case | Filter by student body gender composition |
   200‚Üí
   201‚ÜíA virtual enum backed by two JSONB boolean keys (`data #>> '{nces,men_only}'`
   202‚Üíand `data #>> '{nces,women_only}'`). The `coed` option matches rows where both
   203‚Üíflags are 0 or NULL. This avoids schema changes while providing a clean filter
   204‚Üíinterface.
   205‚Üí
   206‚Üí### MultiEnumField
   207‚Üí
   208‚Üí| Property | Value |
   209‚Üí|----------|-------|
   210‚Üí| FacetKind | `multi_enum` |
   211‚Üí| Filter struct | `MultiEnumFilter { Values []string }` |
   212‚Üí| SQL patterns | Array/any: `ref ?| $N::text[]` |
   213‚Üí| | Array/all: `ref @> $N::jsonb` |
   214‚Üí| | Object-key/any: `ref ?| $N::text[]` |
   215‚Üí| | Object-key/all: `ref ?& $N::text[]` |
   216‚Üí| Use case | Application platforms, fields of study |
   217‚Üí
   218‚ÜíSupports two storage formats (`array`, `object_key`) and two selection modes
   219‚Üí(`any`, `all`), yielding four SQL generation paths. The storage format reflects
   220‚Üíhow the data is stored in the JSONB blob.
   221‚Üí
   222‚Üí### MultiColumnEnumField
   223‚Üí
   224‚Üí| Property | Value |
   225‚Üí|----------|-------|
   226‚Üí| FacetKind | `multi_enum` |
   227‚Üí| Filter struct | `MultiEnumFilter { Values []string }` |
   228‚Üí| SQL pattern | `col = ANY($N::text[])` |
   229‚Üí| Use case | State multi-select |
   230‚Üí
   231‚ÜíUnlike `MultiEnumField` which operates on JSONB, this field targets a direct
   232‚Üícolumn. The filter matches rows where the column value is any one of the
   233‚Üíprovided choices.
   234‚Üí
   235‚Üí---
   236‚Üí
   237‚Üí## Field Sources
   238‚Üí
   239‚Üí`FieldSource` describes where in `search_base.dataset_merged` a field's value
   240‚Üílives.
   241‚Üí
   242‚Üí| SourceKind | SQLRef() Output | Description |
   243‚Üí|------------|----------------|-------------|
   244‚Üí| `column` | `column_name` | Direct top-level column (e.g., `unit_id`, `city`, `state`) |
   245‚Üí| `tsvector` | `column_name` | PostgreSQL TSVector column for full-text search |
   246‚Üí| `jsonb_path` | `data #>> '{k1,k2,...}'` | Path into the `data` JSONB column; extracts as text |
   247‚Üí| `geo` | `column_name` | PostGIS geography column (`coordinates`) |
   248‚Üí
   249‚ÜíFor JSONB paths, `SQLRefAs(castType)` wraps the reference in a PostgreSQL
   250‚Üícast: `(data #>> '{k1,k2}')::integer`. The `JSONBValueType` enum
   251‚Üí(`text`, `numeric`, `integer`, `boolean`, `array`, `object`) determines the
   252‚Üíappropriate cast.
   253‚Üí
   254‚Üí---
   255‚Üí
   256‚Üí## Themes
   257‚Üí
   258‚ÜíThemes group fields into UI sections. The 18 themes match the
   259‚Üí`search_base.theme` PostgreSQL enum and are bound to the GraphQL `Theme` enum
   260‚Üívia `gqlgen.yml`.
   261‚Üí
   262‚Üí| Constant | UI Label | Example Fields |
   263‚Üí|----------|----------|---------------|
   264‚Üí| `CORE` | Core | name, unit_id |
   265‚Üí| `LOCATION` | Location | city, state, location (geo), locale, region, metro_area, metro_distance |
   266‚Üí| `ACADEMICS` | Academics | institution_type, highest_degree, enrollment, student_faculty_ratio, gender, online_only |
   267‚Üí| `COST` | Cost & Financial Aid | avg_net_price, tuition_in_state, tuition_out_of_state, room_and_board, pell_grant_rate, endowment |
   268‚Üí| `OUTCOMES` | Outcomes | admission_rate, graduation_rate, retention_rate, transfer_rate |
   269‚Üí| `RANKING` | Rankings | rank_us_news_national, rank_forbes, rank_niche, score_money |
   270‚Üí| `ADMISSIONS` | Admissions | application_platforms |
   271‚Üí| `ATHLETICS` | Athletics | ncaa_division, ncaa_conference |
   272‚Üí| `FIELDS_OF_STUDY` | Fields of Study | fields_of_study (CIP codes) |
   273‚Üí| `CULTURAL_ENVIRONMENT` | Cultural Environment | partisan_direction, partisan_score, congressional_district |
   274‚Üí| `WEATHER` | Weather & Climate | koppen_climate, koppen_temperature, koppen_precipitation |
   275‚Üí| `PHYSICAL_ENVIRONMENT` | Physical Environment | (reserved) |
   276‚Üí| `DEMOGRAPHICS` | Demographics | (reserved) |
   277‚Üí| `REPUTATION` | Reputation | (reserved) |
   278‚Üí| `SAFETY` | Safety | (reserved) |
   279‚Üí| `STUDENT_LIFE` | Student Life | (reserved) |
   280‚Üí| `METADATA` | Metadata | (reserved) |
   281‚Üí| `OTHER` | Other | (reserved) |
   282‚Üí

   284‚Üí
   285‚Üí## Data Sources
   286‚Üí
   287‚ÜíEach field references a `DataSource` via its `DataSourceID`. The catalog of 12
   288‚Üísources:
   289‚Üí
   290‚Üí| ID | Name | Short | Dataset Key | Description |
   291‚Üí|----|------|-------|-------------|-------------|

   305‚Üí---
   306‚Üí
   307‚Üí## SQL Generation
   308‚Üí
   309‚Üí### Query Pipeline
   310‚Üí
   311‚Üí1. **Validation** -- `ValidateRequest()` checks all filters, pagination bounds,
   312‚Üí   sort field validity, geo radius limits, enum option membership, blend
   313‚Üí   weights, and user location coordinates. All errors are collected (no
   314‚Üí   short-circuit).
   315‚Üí
   316‚Üí2. **WHERE clause assembly** -- `buildWhere()` iterates over `FilterInput`
   317‚Üí   items, looks up each field in the registry, calls `BuildSQL()`, and
   318‚Üí   concatenates the resulting boolean expressions with AND. Placeholder indices
   319‚Üí   (`$1`, `$2`, ...) are advanced globally across all filters.
   320‚Üí
   321‚Üí3. **SELECT construction** -- `BuildQuery()` assembles the full SELECT
   322‚Üí   statement with 10 columns: `unit_id`, `institution_name`, `city`, `state`,
   323‚Üí   `latitude`, `longitude`, distance expression, myPrice expression, `data`
   324‚Üí   (JSONB), and blend score.
   325‚Üí
   326‚Üí4. **Computed columns**:
   327‚Üí   - **Distance**: `ST_Distance(coordinates, ST_SetSRID(ST_MakePoint($lng, $lat), 4326)::geography) / 1609.344`
   328‚Üí     (metres to miles). Only present when `UserLocation` is provided.
   329‚Üí   - **MyPrice**: `CASE WHEN state = $state THEN in_state_tuition ELSE out_of_state_tuition END`.
   330‚Üí     Only present when `UserLocation.State` is non-empty.
   331‚Üí   - **BlendScore**: Weighted normalized average across ranking fields (see
   332‚Üí     Ranking Blend below). Only present when `Blend` is provided.
   333‚Üí
   334‚Üí5. **ORDER BY** -- When a blend is active, results are ordered by blend score
   335‚Üí   ascending (lower is better). Otherwise, `buildOrderBy()` handles built-in
   336‚Üí   sorts (`distance` by column position 7, `name` by `institution_name`) and
   337‚Üí   registry field sorts (column or JSONB-cast reference). NULL handling:
   338‚Üí   `NULLS LAST` for ASC, `NULLS FIRST` for DESC.
   339‚Üí
   340‚Üí6. **Pagination** -- `LIMIT $N OFFSET $M` with defaults (limit=20, max=100).
   341‚Üí   The total offset+limit is capped at `MaxLimit` (100) to prevent deep
   342‚Üí   pagination.
   343‚Üí
   344‚Üí### Query Variants
   345‚Üí
   346‚Üí| Function | Purpose | Differences from BuildQuery |
   347‚Üí|----------|---------|---------------------------|
   348‚Üí| `BuildQuery()` | Primary search | Full pagination (limit + offset) |
   349‚Üí| `BuildCountQuery()` | Total result count | `SELECT COUNT(*)`, no ORDER BY / LIMIT / OFFSET |
   350‚Üí| `BuildExportQuery()` | CSV export | No offset; limit capped by `maxRows` parameter |
   351‚Üí| `BuildInstitutionQuery()` | Single institution detail | Hardcoded columns; `WHERE unit_id = $1` |
   352‚Üí
   353‚Üí### Parameterization
   354‚Üí
   355‚ÜíAll user-supplied values flow through `$N` placeholders. Field values, sort
   356‚Üíparameters, pagination limits, user coordinates, and state codes are all bound
   357‚Üías query arguments. SQL expressions use `fmt.Sprintf` only for column
   358‚Üíreferences and structural SQL -- never for user data.
   359‚Üí
   360‚Üí---
   361‚Üí
   362‚Üí## Ranking Blend
   363‚Üí
   364‚ÜíThe blend system computes a weighted composite score across multiple ranking
   365‚Üíproviders. Seven providers are eligible:
   366‚Üí
   367‚Üí| Field Name | Max Value | Direction |
   368‚Üí|------------|-----------|-----------|
   369‚Üí| `rank_us_news_national` | 440 | Lower is better |
   370‚Üí| `rank_us_news_liberal_arts` | 230 | Lower is better |
   371‚Üí| `rank_forbes` | 500 | Lower is better |
   372‚Üí| `rank_washington_monthly_national` | 400 | Lower is better |
   373‚Üí| `rank_washington_monthly_liberal_arts` | 250 | Lower is better |
   374‚Üí| `rank_niche` | 1600 | Lower is better |
   375‚Üí| `score_money` | 5.0 | Higher is better |
   376‚Üí
   377‚Üí### Normalization
   378‚Üí
   379‚ÜíEach ranking value is normalized to [0, 1] where 0 is best:
   380‚Üí
   381‚Üí- **Lower-is-better (ranks)**: `COALESCE((ref)::numeric / maxVal, 1.0)`
   382‚Üí- **Higher-is-better (scores)**: `COALESCE(1.0 - (ref)::numeric / maxVal, 1.0)`
   383‚Üí
   384‚ÜíNULL values normalize to 1.0 (worst), penalizing unranked institutions.
   385‚Üí
   386‚Üí### Composite Expression
   387‚Üí
   388‚Üí```sql
   389‚Üí(w1 * norm1 + w2 * norm2 + ...) / (w1 + w2 + ...)
   390‚Üí```
   391‚Üí
   392‚ÜíThe expression uses only constants (no parameterized args) and is embedded
   393‚Üídirectly in the SELECT list. When a blend is active, results are ordered by
   394‚Üí`blend_score ASC NULLS LAST`.
   395‚Üí
   396‚Üí---
   397‚Üí
   398‚Üí## Similar Institutions
   399‚Üí
   400‚Üí`BuildSimilarInstitutionsQuery()` finds institutions most similar to a given
   401‚Üíone using Euclidean distance across normalized dimensions:
   402‚Üí
   403‚Üí- **Min-max normalized**: enrollment (log-scale), tuition (in-state and
   404‚Üí  out-of-state), ACT midpoint, SAT math midpoint
   405‚Üí- **Rate dimensions** (already 0-1): graduation rate, retention rate,
   406‚Üí  admission rate
   407‚Üí- **Categorical penalty**: +0.09 when institution type (control) differs
   408‚Üí
   409‚ÜíThe query uses a CTE to compute dataset-wide min/max statistics, retrieves the
   410‚Üísource institution's values, then computes `|/(sum of squared differences)` as
   411‚Üíthe Euclidean distance. Results are ordered by distance ascending with a
   412‚Üíconfigurable limit (default 10, max 50).
   413‚Üí
   414‚Üí---
   415‚Üí
   416‚Üí## Validation
   417‚Üí
   418‚Üí`ValidateRequest()` performs comprehensive input validation:
   419‚Üí
   420‚Üí| Check | Sentinel Field | Rule |
   421‚Üí|-------|---------------|------|
   422‚Üí| Unknown field name | field name | Must exist in registry |
   423‚Üí| Filter JSON structure | field name | Must deserialize to the field's expected filter struct |
   424‚Üí| Text length | field name | Full-text, substring, exact-string queries capped at 512 characters |
   425‚Üí| Numeric range | field name | At least one of min/max required; values within hard bounds |
   426‚Üí| Enum membership | field name | Value must appear in field's Options (when Options is non-nil) |
   427‚Üí| Multi-enum count | field name | At least one value required |
   428‚Üí| Geo radius | field name | Must be positive; must not exceed `MaxRadiusKm` (500 km) |
   429‚Üí| Geo kind | field name | Must be `radius` or `bbox` |
   430‚Üí| Pagination limit | `_limit` | Must not exceed `MaxLimit` (100) |
   431‚Üí| Pagination offset | `_offset` | Must be non-negative |
   432‚Üí| User location | `_userLocation` | Latitude in [-90, 90]; longitude in [-180, 180]; state is 2-letter uppercase |
   433‚Üí| Blend weights | `_blend` | Field must be a known ranking provider; weight in [0, 100]; at least one non-zero |
   434‚Üí| Sort field | `_sort` | Must be `distance`, `name`, or a sortable registry field; distance requires userLocation |
   435‚Üí
   436‚ÜíAll errors are collected into a `[]ValidationError` slice. Validation never
   437‚Üíshort-circuits -- callers receive every problem at once.
   438‚Üí
   439‚Üí---
   440‚Üí
   441‚Üí## Registry
   442‚Üí
   443‚Üí`NewRegistry()` populates a `map[string]Field` from the `allFields()` function
   444‚Üíin `registry.go`. The registry provides:
   445‚Üí
   446‚Üí| Method | Returns | Purpose |
   447‚Üí|--------|---------|---------|
   448‚Üí| `Lookup(name)` | `(Field, bool)` | O(1) field lookup by name |
   449‚Üí| `All()` | `[]Field` | All registered fields (unordered) |
   450‚Üí| `Descriptors()` | `[]FieldDescriptor` | Metadata for every field |
   451‚Üí| `OutputDescriptors()` | `[]FieldDescriptor` | Fields with `OutputDefault != nil` (result columns) |
   452‚Üí| `ExportableDescriptors()` | `[]FieldDescriptor` | Exportable output fields, ordered by theme then registration order |
   453‚Üí
   454‚Üí---
   455‚Üí
   456‚Üí## Adding a New Field
   457‚Üí
   458‚Üí1. Add a field struct literal to the `allFields()` slice in `registry.go`.
   459‚Üí   Every field must explicitly set `visibility`, `searchability`, and
   460‚Üí   `exportable` -- there are no defaults.
   461‚Üí
   462‚Üí2. The field's `Name()` becomes the filter key in API requests and the
   463‚Üí   metadata endpoint.
   464‚Üí
   465‚Üí3. Choose the appropriate concrete type (`NumericRangeField`,
   466‚Üí   `SingleEnumField`, etc.) or create a new one if no existing type fits.
   467‚Üí
   468‚Üí4. If creating a new field type, implement the `Field` interface including
   469‚Üí   `BuildSQL()` in `field.go`.
   470‚Üí
   471‚Üí5. New fields are automatically covered by the table-driven
   472‚Üí   `TestBuildSQLAllFields` test in `search_test.go`. Add targeted test cases
   473‚Üí   for edge cases or new field types.
   474‚Üí
   475‚Üí6. Run `make api-test` to verify. For JSONB-backed fields, also run
   476‚Üí   integration tests against a real database to catch type encoding issues.
   477‚Üí
   478‚Üí---

   148‚Üí## Provider Composition
   149‚Üí
   150‚ÜíThe application is wrapped in providers in `main.tsx`, from outermost to
   151‚Üíinnermost:
   152‚Üí
   153‚Üí1. **`StrictMode`** -- React development checks
   154‚Üí2. **`UrqlProvider`** -- GraphQL client (urql with auth exchange)
   155‚Üí3. **`MantineProvider`** -- UI theme and component styles
   156‚Üí4. **`BrowserRouter`** -- Client-side routing
   157‚Üí5. **`App`** -- Application shell (header, routes, footer)

   161‚Üí## Pages and Routing
   162‚Üí
   163‚ÜíFour routes defined in `App.tsx`:
   164‚Üí
   165‚Üí| Path | Component | Description |
   166‚Üí|------|-----------|-------------|
   167‚Üí| `/` | `Home` | Landing page with sample search carousel |
   168‚Üí| `/search` | `Search` | Faceted search interface with multiple result views |
   169‚Üí| `/about` | `About` | Information page |
   170‚Üí| `/auth/callback` | `AuthCallback` | OAuth redirect handler (Supabase) |
   171‚Üí
   172‚ÜíAll pages share a common layout: `Header` (top) and `Footer` (bottom), with
   173‚Üíthe page content in a flex column between them.
   174‚Üí
   175‚Üí---
   176‚Üí
   177‚Üí## State Management
   178‚Üí
   179‚Üí### URL-Driven Search State
   180‚Üí
   181‚ÜíThe search page encodes its full state in URL query parameters:
   182‚Üí
   183‚Üí- Active filters (by field name)
   184‚Üí- Pagination (page number, page size)
   185‚Üí- Sort field and direction
   186‚Üí- View mode (card, list, table, map)
   187‚Üí- Ranking blend weights
   188‚Üí
   189‚ÜíThis makes search results bookmarkable and shareable. The URL is the single
   190‚Üísource of truth for search state.
   191‚Üí
   192‚Üí### Zustand Stores
   193‚Üí
   194‚Üí#### authStore (`stores/authStore.ts`)
   195‚Üí
   196‚Üí| Field | Type | Description |
   197‚Üí|-------|------|-------------|
   198‚Üí| `session` | `Session \| null` | Current Supabase session |
   199‚Üí| `user` | `User \| null` | Current Supabase user |
   200‚Üí| `loading` | `boolean` | True during initial session check |
   201‚Üí
   202‚Üí| Action | Description |
   203‚Üí|--------|-------------|
   204‚Üí| `initialize()` | Loads session from Supabase, subscribes to auth state changes |
   205‚Üí| `setSession(session)` | Updates session and user |
   206‚Üí| `signOut()` | Calls `supabase.auth.signOut()`, clears state |
   207‚Üí| `getToken()` | Returns `session.access_token` or `undefined` |
   208‚Üí
   209‚Üí#### preferencesStore (`stores/preferencesStore.ts`)
   210‚Üí
   211‚Üí| Field | Type | Description |
   212‚Üí|-------|------|-------------|
   213‚Üí| `homeLatitude` | `number \| null` | User's home latitude |
   214‚Üí| `homeLongitude` | `number \| null` | User's home longitude |
   215‚Üí| `homeState` | `string \| null` | User's home state |
   216‚Üí| `loaded` | `boolean` | Whether preferences have been fetched |
   217‚Üí| `loading` | `boolean` | Whether a fetch is in progress |
   218‚Üí
   219‚Üí| Action | Description |
   220‚Üí|--------|-------------|
   221‚Üí| `load()` | Fetches preferences from GraphQL API (guarded against duplicate loads) |
   222‚Üí| `update(prefs)` | Sends mutation to update preferences on server |
   223‚Üí| `clear()` | Resets all fields to null/false (called on sign-out) |
   224‚Üí
   225‚Üí### localStorage Persistence
   226‚Üí
   227‚ÜíSome UI preferences are persisted directly in localStorage (not via Zustand):
   228‚Üí
   229‚Üí- Column visibility settings (table view)
   230‚Üí- Column order (table view)
   231‚Üí- View mode preference (card/list/table/map)
   232‚Üí
   233‚Üí### Initialization Flow
   234‚Üí
   235‚ÜíOn application mount (`App.tsx`):
   236‚Üí
   237‚Üí1. `authStore.initialize()` is called, which:
   238‚Üí   - Fetches the current session from Supabase
   239‚Üí   - Subscribes to `onAuthStateChange` for future updates
   240‚Üí2. When `session` becomes non-null (user is logged in):
   241‚Üí   - `preferencesStore.load()` fetches user preferences from the API
   242‚Üí3. When `session` becomes null (user logs out):
   243‚Üí   - `preferencesStore.clear()` resets preferences
   244‚Üí
   245‚Üí---
   246‚Üí
   247‚Üí## GraphQL Integration
   248‚Üí
   249‚Üí### Client Setup (`lib/urqlClient.ts`)
   250‚Üí
   251‚ÜíThe urql client is configured with three exchanges:
   252‚Üí
   253‚Üí1. **`cacheExchange`** -- Default document cache
   254‚Üí2. **`authExchange`** -- JWT attachment and refresh (see [Authentication spec](authentication.md))
   255‚Üí3. **`fetchExchange`** -- HTTP transport
   256‚Üí
   257‚ÜíThe client targets `/graphql` (relative URL), which the Vite dev server proxies
   258‚Üíto `localhost:8081` during development.
   259‚Üí
   260‚Üí### Schema-Aware Typing (gql.tada)
   261‚Üí
   262‚ÜíAll GraphQL queries use `graphql()` from gql.tada, which provides compile-time
   263‚Üítype checking against the schema. The workflow:
   264‚Üí
   265‚Üí1. API schema is defined in `api/graph/schema/*.graphql`
   266‚Üí2. `make web-schema-sync` copies the SDL to `web/schema.graphql` and regenerates
   267‚Üí   `web/src/graphql-env.d.ts`
   268‚Üí3. TypeScript checks queries against the schema via `@0no-co/graphqlsp`
   269‚Üí4. `ResultOf<typeof MyQuery>` extracts strongly-typed result types
   270‚Üí
   271‚ÜíPlain string GraphQL queries are prohibited. The only exception is the
   272‚Üípreferences store, which uses raw strings for simplicity (pre-dating gql.tada
   273‚Üíadoption).
   274‚Üí
   275‚Üí---

   329‚Üí## Search Interface (`src/pages/Search.tsx`)
   330‚Üí
   331‚ÜíThe search page is the primary feature, providing faceted search over college
   332‚Üídata with multiple result views.
   333‚Üí
   334‚Üí### Layout
   335‚Üí
   336‚Üí```
   337‚Üí+--------------------------------------------------+
   338‚Üí|  Header                                          |
   339‚Üí+----------+---------------------------------------+
   340‚Üí|          |  View Toggle  |  Export  |  Sort      |
   341‚Üí|  Filters |---------------------------------------+
   342‚Üí|  (by     |                                       |
   343‚Üí|  theme)  |  Results                              |
   344‚Üí|          |  (Card / List / Table / Map)           |
   345‚Üí|  Location|                                       |
   346‚Üí|  Input   |                                       |
   347‚Üí|          |                                       |
   348‚Üí|  Ranking |---------------------------------------+
   349‚Üí|  Blend   |  Pagination                           |
   350‚Üí+----------+---------------------------------------+
   351‚Üí|  Footer                                          |
   352‚Üí+--------------------------------------------------+
   353‚Üí```
   354‚Üí
   355‚Üí### Responsive Behavior
   356‚Üí
   357‚Üí- **Desktop** (>= 62em): Sidebar always visible alongside results
   358‚Üí- **Mobile** (< 62em): Filters collapse into a drawer, toggled by a button
   359‚Üí
   360‚Üí### Result Views
   361‚Üí
   362‚Üí| View | Component | Features |
   363‚Üí|------|-----------|----------|
   364‚Üí| Card | `InstitutionCard` | Visual cards in a grid layout |
   365‚Üí| List | `ResultsList` | Compact list with key data points |
   366‚Üí| Table | `ResultsTable` | Sortable columns, customizable via `ColumnPicker` |
   367‚Üí| Map | `ResultsMap` | Leaflet map with institution markers |
   368‚Üí
   369‚ÜíView selection is persisted in localStorage and encoded in the URL.
   370‚Üí
   371‚Üí### Filter System
   372‚Üí
   373‚ÜíFilters are organized by theme (matching the API's `search.Theme` enum).
   374‚Üí`FilterControl` is the main component that renders the appropriate input for
   375‚Üíeach field type:
   376‚Üí
   377‚Üí| Field Type | UI Control |
   378‚Üí|------------|------------|
   379‚Üí| Full-text search | Text input with debounce (400ms) |
   380‚Üí| Substring | Text input with debounce (400ms) |
   381‚Üí| Single enum | Dropdown select |
   382‚Üí| Multi enum | Checkbox group (`MultiSelectCheckboxes`) |
   383‚Üí| Numeric range | Min/max number inputs |
   384‚Üí| Geo | Lat/lng inputs + radius slider (via `GeoFilterControl`) |
   385‚Üí| Ranking | Numeric range inputs |
   386‚Üí
   387‚Üí### Ranking Blend Builder
   388‚Üí
   389‚Üí`RankingBlendBuilder` allows users to create custom composite rankings by
   390‚Üíassigning weights to individual ranking sources. The builder:
   391‚Üí
   392‚Üí1. Displays available rankings as sliders (0-100 weight)
   393‚Üí2. Computes a weighted blend on the server side
   394‚Üí3. Sorts results by the blended score
   395‚Üí
   396‚Üí### Similar Institutions
   397‚Üí
   398‚Üí`SimilarInstitutions` shows institutions similar to a selected one, providing
   399‚Üídiscovery beyond filter-based search.

   431‚Üí### Test Helpers
   432‚Üí
   433‚Üí| Helper | Location | Purpose |
   434‚Üí|--------|----------|---------|

   436‚Üí| `setup.ts` | `src/test/setup.ts` | Extends Vitest with jest-dom matchers, auto-cleanup |
   437‚Üí
   438‚Üí### Testing Patterns
   439‚Üí
   440‚Üí1. **Fixtures first**: Define field objects matching GraphQL types at top of test files
   441‚Üí2. **User events**: Use `userEvent.setup()` for realistic interactions
   442‚Üí3. **Debounce handling**: Use `waitFor(..., { timeout: 1000 })` for debounced callbacks
   443‚Üí4. **Accessibility queries**: Prefer `getByRole()` over `getByLabelText()`
   444‚Üí5. **Mock GraphQL**: Use `vi.mock('urql', ...)` with operation name dispatch
